# Figure description: Схема_изоглубин_по_Т3.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/plan_data/Схема_изоглубин_по_Т3.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isodepth (isoglubina) map** — specifically a contour map of structural depths to a seismic reflection horizon, displayed as an isoline (contour) map with depth values labeled on contour lines. It is analogous to a structure contour map used in petroleum and mineral exploration to depict the subsurface configuration of a key stratigraphic or structural marker horizon. The map also incorporates a color-intensity (shading) overlay representing depth intensity zones.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large region in **northern Kazakhstan**, compiled from multiple map sheets at **1:500,000 scale**. The sheet codes listed in the title block include:

- **N-42**: sheets 126-БГ, 128-Г, 128-Г, 129-БГ, 137-БГ, 138, 139, 140, 141
- **M-42**: sheets 3-БГ, 4, III, 7-АБ, 8-А, Б, 9, 15, 18
- **VII (8.П)**, **VIII (3.П)**, **IX (3.П)**, **51**, **52**, **XV**, **44-А, Б**

The subject is the **reference (key) seismic reflection horizon designated "T₂"** (опорный отражающий горизонт Т₂), which likely corresponds to the top of a major stratigraphic unit (probably Paleozoic basement or a significant intra-sedimentary boundary within the Tenizkaya (Тенизская) depression/basin).

The figure is specifically referenced as **Чертёж № 168** (Drawing No. 168) and is tied to the report of the **Zapadno-Tenizkaya (Western Teniz) Party, 1978–84**.

The geographic structural framework shown is the **Тенизская впадина** (Teniz Depression/Basin), a major sedimentary basin in central Kazakhstan.

---

## Key Content

**Contour Pattern and Depth Values:**
- The isodepth contours show **depth to the T₂ horizon** in meters, with contour values visible ranging from approximately **500 m to over 2000 m** (values such as 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 1000, 1100, 1200, 1500, 1800, 2000+ are legible on contour lines).
- The contour interval appears to be **100 m** in shallower zones and possibly 200–500 m in deeper zones.
- The map reveals a **complex structural configuration** with multiple **closed highs (uplifts/domes)** shown as concentric oval-to-circular contour closures — these represent structural highs where the T₂ horizon is relatively shallow — and **intervening lows/depressions** where contours open outward indicating greater depth.

**Color/Shading Overlay:**
- A **pink-to-red color gradient** (intensity shading) is applied across portions of the map. According to the legend ("Шкала интенсивности изоглубин, в м" — Scale of isodepth intensity, in meters), the shading represents depth intensity:
  - **Light pink**: shallower depths (approximately 500–800 m)
  - **Medium pink/salmon**: intermediate depths (~800–1000 m)
  - **Deeper pink/red**: greater depths (>1000 m, up to 1500+ m)
- The deepest (most intensely colored red/dark) areas appear in the **southern and southeastern portions** of the map, and in some localized depressions between structural highs.
- **Cross-hatched (diagonal ruling) zones** appear around the margins and in specific areas — these represent zones where the T₂ horizon **crops out at or near the surface**, or zones outside the basin boundary.

**Structural Features:**
- Several **prominent closed structural highs** are visible as concentric contour patterns, particularly in the central and southwestern portions of the map. These are likely named local structures but individual structure names are not clearly legible at this resolution.
- The **Teniz Depression boundary** (Граница Тенизской впадины) is shown as a distinct boundary line separating the basin interior from the surrounding uplifted areas.
- The northeastern portion of the map shows a distinct **separate structural block** (upper right inset area) which may represent a separate sub-basin or platform area.

**Legend Symbols (Условные обозначения):**
1. **Изоглубины** — Isodepth contour lines
2. **Зоны потери корреляции отраженных волн, преимущественно связываемые с разрывными нарушениями или с крутым залеганием слоев** — Zones of loss of reflection correlation, primarily associated with faults or steep dip of strata (shown with a specific hatch pattern)
3. **Граница Тенизской впадины** — Boundary of the Teniz Depression
4. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты и более древних пород по сейсмическим данным ДГРЗ** — Surface exposures of the Vladimirskaya Suite deposits and older rocks, based on DGRZ seismic data
5. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты по сейсмическим данным** — Surface exposures of Vladimirskaya Suite deposits based on seismic data
6. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты и более древних пород по геологическим данным** — Surface exposures of Vladimirskaya Suite and older rocks based on geological data

**Sheet Layout Diagram:**
A small **index diagram** ("Схема расположения листов") in the lower right shows the arrangement of the constituent map sheets (labeled with Roman numerals I–XV and sub-designations), confirming the multi-sheet compilation nature of this map.
